You can replay the battle manually, overriding autobattle results.
Replay the battle manually, overriding autobattle results.
Accept autobattle results, including all depicted losses.
Flee from battle. The hero will be available for hire in the Tavern, but their army will be lost forever.
Saving… Please do not close the game.
The hero increases the Attack of all creatures in their army by the value of their own Attack attribute. This bonus directly affects the Damage those creatures deal in battle.
The hero increases the Defense of all creatures in their army by the value of their own Defense attribute. This reduces the Damage those creatures take in battle.
Enhances the strength of the hero’s spells, increasing both their Damage and the duration of applied effects.
Increases the hero’s maximum mana by 10 for each point of Knowledge.
The hero increases the Luck of all creatures in their army by the value of their own Luck attribute. This affects the chance of those creatures landing a Lucky or Unlucky strike.
The hero increases the Morale of all creatures in their army by the value of their own Morale attribute. This affects the chance to take an extra turn or skip a turn.
Spent by the hero to move on the global map. When the hero runs out of movement points, they can no longer move. The cost per space depends on the terrain and direction of movement.
The hero’s current amount of experience, which is required to level up.
Used to cast spells in battle and on the global map. Some is restored at the start of each day.
At the start of each battle, Schism creatures grow stronger based on the current Communion level.

Determines the percentage of the strongest enemy creature that will be raised as Undead. If your army has no Undead of that tier, or if raising Undead is disabled, fallen enemies are converted into Necromantic Energy instead.
Increases Damage dealt with basic attacks.
The final Damage also depends on the target’s Defense: DM = [20 + attacker’s Attack] / [20 + target’s Defense] Minimum modifier: 0
Reduces Damage taken from basic attacks.
The final Damage also depends on the attacker’s Attack: DM = [20 + attacker’s Attack] / [20 + target’s Defense] Minimum modifier: 0

Determines the base Damage dealt by the stack.

The amount of Damage (minimum to maximum) a single unit in the stack deals.
Gives the stack a chance to take an extra turn (if positive) or skip their turn (if negative).

By default, every point of Morale above zero grants a 4% chance to take an extra turn. By default, every point of Morale below zero grants a 4% chance to skip a turn. Each creature has its own minimum and maximum Morale range based on their creature type and traits.
Gives the stack a chance to deal increased or reduced Damage.

By default, every point of Luck above zero grants a 6% chance to deal 150% Damage. By default, every point of Luck below zero grants a 6% chance to deal 50% Damage. Each creature has its own minimum and maximum Luck based on their creature type and traits.
Determines the turn order in battle. The higher it is, the earlier the stack acts.
Determines the stack’s movement range in battle. The higher it is, the farther they can move. It is also a secondary parameter affecting turn order.

When you play multiplayer, we create an online profile using your Xbox network account so we can handle matchmaking and rankings.
Other players may see your display name, profile picture and basic stats. You can reset or delete your multiplayer profile anytime in the game’s settings.

Gold is the primary resource used for most core actions, such as recruting units and constructing buildings. It can be found while exploring the Global Map and is produced daily by cities and Gold Mines.
Wood is a common construction resource used to build city structures. It can be found while exploring the Global Map and is produced daily by Sawmills.
Ore is a common construction resource used to build city structures. It can be found while exploring the Global Map and is produced daily by Ore Mines.
Gems are a valuable resource required to construct more advanced buildings and recruit powerful units. They can be found while exploring the Global Map and are produced daily by Gem Mounds.
Crystals are a valuable resource required to construct more advanced buildings and recruit powerful units. They can be found while exploring the Global Map and are produced daily by Crystal Veins.
Mercury is a valuable resource required to construct more advanced buildings and recruit powerful units. It can be found while exploring the Global Map and is produced daily by Mercury Fissures.

Alchemical Dust is a special resource used to upgrade artifacts and spells, as well as to construct certain special buildings. It can be found while exploring the Global Map, created by destroying artifacts, or obtained by converting Gems, Crystals, and Mercury in Alchemical Labs.
Grail is a mysterious resource required to construct exceptionally powerful buildings. It can be discovered during your adventures!
All spells of Magic School learned: {0}.

Law points are a special resource used to activate your faction’s unique bonuses. They are generated by your cities and by heroes when they capture Global Map objects and or win battles (20% of the hero’s XP is converted into Law points).

When you accumulate enough points, you earn a Law Seal, which can be spent to enact a Law or saved to unlock more powerful, higher-tier Laws later.

Astrology points are a special resource generated by your cities. They are used to learn high Neutral magic — powerful Global Map spells that also require the hero to meet specific level requirements.

When you accumulate enough points, you gain an Insight, which can be spent to unlock and learn a high Neutral spell.

To earn Law points, develop your cities, fight enemies, and capture objects on the Global Map.
Don’t forget to use your Law Seals to enhance your army and economy.
The Magic Observatory contains all spells available to your heroes.
You can learn a spell if at least one of your cities has a Mage Guild of the required level.
Earn Astrology points to gain an Insight, which is required to learn powerful Global Map spells.
Some Global Map spells can only be used a limited number of times. Their usage limit refreshes at the start of each in‑game day.
Interacting with objects on adjacent tiles does not consume Movement points.
Moving along roads costs fewer Movement points, while moving across difficult terrain costs more.
Each faction has its own unique city, creatures, and hero roster, as well as a native terrain type on the Global Map.
Casting spells in combat and on the Global Map requires mana, which partially regenerates at the start of each new day.
Cities are the foundation of your kingdom. They produce resources, allow you to construct buildings, recruit heroes, and hire creatures.
Alchemical Dust is a rare resource used to upgrade spells and artifacts and to construct certain city buildings.
One way to obtain Alchemical Dust is by destroying artifacts and spell scrolls.
When a hero levels up, they gain random attributes and learn new skills that significantly strengthen them and their army.
If a hero acquires a special combination of skills, they unlock a unique class that grants powerful advantages and can turn the tide even in the hardest matches.
You can level up your spells. Each level makes them stronger and adds new effects.
Artifacts are powerful items that strengthen your hero. Some belong to sets — the more pieces of a set the hero has, the greater bonus they receive.
Neutral squads guard certain objects and areas of the Global Map, as well as portals, preventing the heroes from simply passing through them.
Mirage is a mysterious place. Defeat a stronger version of yourself to claim the Grail!
At the start of each battle, you may freely position your stacks on the battlefield.
Turn order in the battle is determined by creatures’ Initiative. If Initiative is equal, the stack with higher Speed acts first.
Creatures and heroes may use active abilities by spending Focus.
You gain Focus points whenever your creatures attack or take Damage.
Battle spells have cooldowns. After being cast, a spell becomes available again only after several rounds.
At the start of each in-game week, a special event may occur that changes the game rules.
Classic victory condition requires you to defeat all enemy heroes and capture all enemy cities.
Capital capture is a special victory condition that requires you to capture your opponents’ starting cities.
Capital hold is a special victory condition that requires you to capture your opponents’ starting cities and control them for a specified amount of time.
Final battle is a special victory condition that requires you to participate in a major decisive battle. The winner of that battle wins the game.
City hold is a special victory condition that requires you to capture a specific city and control it for a specified amount of time.
Tournament is a special victory condition that requires you to participate in several battles. The player who wins a specified number of those battles wins the game.
You can adjust the game’s visuals by toggling color intensity and additional effects that enhance readability.
Press the Alt key to highlight interactable objects on the Global Map.
Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era tells the stories of the mysterious continent of Jadame!
Although the lands of the Temple of the Sun are divided among lords, barons, and other landholders, all of them are de jure subordinate to the Inquisition Council.
The Jadame Necromancers Guild is not a state. Nevertheless, it readily imposes its own order on territories whose owners cooperate with the Guild.

All creatures of the Grove living in the Murmurwoods are connected to the Mycelium to some degree. Many barely perceive this bond, while others interact with the forest in a far more conscious manner.
Semi-sentient tribes of insectoid creatures formed the foundation of the Hive army on Jadame.
The Abyss is the homeland of the Schism creatures and the lower layer of the Elemental Plane of Water. It is also directly connected to the enigmatic Realm Between Realms.
The Triumvirate is an equal alliance of minotaurs, dark elves, and dragons, jointly ruling Alvar Dominion. Only a portion of them actually reside in the Dungeon.

Hero’s Knowledge increases by 3 until the end of the mission.
Hero’s Knowledge increases by 4 until the end of the mission.
Hero’s Attack, Defense, Spell Power and Knowledge increase by 2 until the end of the mission.
HP of one unit in the stack. When a unit’s HP drops to 0, the stack loses one unit.
The number of units in the stack, which affects the Damage it deals. When this number reaches 0, the stack dies and no longer participates in battle.

Focus is a special resource used to activate creature and hero abilities.
To use an ability, the army spends Focus Charges.
1 Focus Charge = 6 Focus points.

The army generates Focus when its creatures attack or take damage:
• All creatures generate 1 Focus Point upon attacking, except for Melee creatures, which generate 2.
• All creatures generate 1 Focus Point upon taking Damage.
Victory conditions are defined by the map’s scenario.
Defeat all enemy heroes and capture every enemy city to win.
Capture the opponent’s starting city to win.
Capture the opponent’s starting city and hold it for a set number of in-game days to win.
Face the opponent’s hero in a final battle. The winner claims victory in the game.
Capture the marked neutral city and hold it for a set number of in-game days to win.
Face the opponent’s hero in a series of battles. The winner of the set claims victory in the game.
Capture the opponent’s starting city to trigger the final battle with their hero. The winner claims victory in the game.
A classic scenario where you must defeat all your opponents.
Best for singleplayer. Defeat both your Light and Dark sides on the path to glory!
Best for singleplayer. Defend the people of these lands against the enemy raids!
Best for singleplayer. You are the lord of the last foothold. Protect it from approaching enemies and defend your capital.
Best for singleplayer. Defeat the enemies surrounding you before they grow strong enough to destroy the world!
Best for singleplayer. Survive between a rock and a hard place while the lords around you tear each other apart.
Best for singleplayer. Set out on an adventure full of danger and discoveries! Face the enemy hero and defeat them.
Best for singleplayer. Pass judgement on two outlaws hiding in these lands. But keep your eyes wide open — evil is rising!
Best for singleplayer. Choose your path wisely, for only one leads to victory! Beware the vagabonds roaming the roads, for you must defeat them.
Best for singleplayer. Withstand the onslaught of multiple enemies as a solitary adventurer, and prove you are the strongest among them!
Best for singleplayer. Reach the top, confront your opponents, and crush everyone you meet along the way!
This template is filled with incredible treasures! Reach the central area before your opponent to claim them — and secure victory in the final battle.
Defeat the opponent’s hero and seize their starting city to secure victory in the final battle. Protect your own capital at all costs!
Challenge your opponent in the Hell Light Arena, or wait until the new month begins to be drawn into battle automatically. The winner takes it all!
Develop your cities and strengthen your army to defeat the opponent in a series of weekly battles.
Outer dwellings do not provide weekly growth — manage your army carefully and defeat your opponent!
Build your economy, develop your cities, and set out on an adventure and defeat your opponents in the race for power!
You have just 28 days and several battles with the opponent. Focus on city development and efficiently building your army to secure the victory!
Venture into untamed lands brimming with riches — claim your rewards and face your opponent at the end of the journey!
Many heroes, but only one will be chosen! Prepare your starting hero as a champion to defend your honor in the tournament!
Race to the forgotten lands filled with immense treasure! Outsmart your opponent to claim victory.
Defend your last fortress — losing it means losing the game! Fight through enemy forces and expand your lands.
Fight your opponent in a tournament until one of you wins three times. Venture into the dangerous areas of your lands to gain an advantage!
Set out on a twisted treasure hunt! Outsmart your opponent, capture their capital, and hold it to claim the victory!
Dive into complete chaos — no limits, broken rules and glitched terrain await! Experience Heroes of Might and Magic from an entirely new perspective!
Journey across the holiday tree, gathering treasures from Pandora’s Boxes dangling on its branches. Face your opponent in battle, and celebrate your victory!
Conquer the entire map with the army of your native city, advancing steadily through multiple territories. A multitude of riches and adventures await!
Choose your path wisely — each one holds treasures, some greater than others!
Treasure awaits in the nearby lands — but you’re not the only one seeking it! Defeat your opponents, or you’ll be leaving empty-handed!
A scarce but exciting template, limited in resources, artifacts, and objects. Fight multiple opponents, develop steadily, and conquer the lands!
You can have as many heroes as the number of cities you control — fair and square! Conquer more lands to build a mighty party and defeat your opponents.
Your cities are separated by lands full of monsters. Try to build connections between them, unite your armies, claim the treasures, and defeat all enemies!
The more opponents, the merrier! Be the first to reach “the Sun,” the central area of the template where enormous treasures await.
A rich template designed for playing with a unifaction army. Assemble a mighty force from units found in Pandora’s Boxes and face your opponent in the center
A scarce template tailored for an intense game of 3–6 players. Defeat your neighbor to advance towards the center — reach it first for the best chance to win!
Enemies all around! To become the King of the Hill in this scarce template’s central area, you’ll need to fight your way through relentless battles.
This template has many different areas to explore! However, there are no roads leading to the treasure deposits — you’ll have to find your own way in!
Defeat the nearby AI opponents and claim the treasures they guard before facing your final enemy.
Scarce resources and little gold. Your opponent will need a miracle to win… and so will you.
Use your faction’s strengths and see who comes out on top! Break into the center with your native army only, and crush your enemy like grain in a windmill.
Want to play in a big group of friends? Then this 8-player template is just for you! Collect every item, break into the center, and join the battle for victory!
Climb to the top of the pyramid, where great treasures await! However, you will have to fight not just one, but two opponents to claim them!
Seven opponents, no resources, no gold… How can anyone win in such harsh conditions? Your wit and luck will be put to the ultimate test!
This template resembles a spider’s web, containing multiple areas and opponents. Expect no great riches, and be careful not to get lost within.
A rich template that offers three paths to reach your enemy. The shorter the path, the greater the dangers — and the treasures — that await you.
A rich template that offers three paths to reach your enemy. The shorter the path, the greater the dangers — and the treasures — that await you.
You have obtained scrolls of the essential Global Map spells. Use them to conquer the lands, collect all treasures in the central area, and crush your opponent!
The war never ends… or does it? Defeat the guards of the central area, where everyone wishes to join you without hesitation.
A month of plague has devastated these lands. To gather resources and survive, you must expand outward and conquer distant territories.
Show off your diplomatic skills and gather an undefeatable army on this enormous template!
Tired of unfair map generation? Prove your skill on this arena-style template where both players start with nearly identical areas.
Obstacle course: make your way to the richest areas, battling your opponent along the way. Whoever earns more victories becomes the sprint champion!
A drought has struck Jadame. Rescue the grolls and find them a new refuge in the oasis! But beware — someone else would love to claim the paradise.
Fully built cities, massive armies, and max-level heroes. All the cards are in your hands — now prove you’re the strongest!
A simple template for fast-paced, time-limited play. Dive into a frantic confrontation and fight to emerge victorious!
Will you reach a dead end, encounter an enemy, or uncover treasure? Navigate this roadless labyrinth and recruit as many troops as you can along the way!
Nothing is more classic than this symphony. Rely only on yourself and your starting city, for no other help will come.
Complete Act I of the campaign.
Complete Act II of the campaign.
Complete Act III of the campaign.
Ensure at least one minotaur survives at the end of the Garner Hills mission.
Recruit all Kittenhorn squads during the Into the Woods mission.
Recruit Infernal Hydras during the Into the Woods mission.
Solve the mystery of the Shard of Serenity during the Neverending Conflict mission.
Pursue and catch up with the warlock during the Burning Hearts mission.
Defeat Old Pilgrim during the Burning Hearts mission.
Resist the Dragonfly King’s herald during the Deal with a Vampire mission.
Resist the Dragonfly King’s herald during the Saving the Knight mission.
Save an angel during the Corruption mission.
Save all Alvarian peasants during the Alvar Diruil mission.
Use the pixies’ scrolls like PROPERLY during the Neverending Conflict mission.
Forget to fulfill your promise to the Troglodyte in the Saving the Knight mission.
Destroy all the amplifiers in the Renegade mission.
Exploring the world in the classic mode
Playing Deal with a Vampire mission
Preparing for battle in the Arena
